zh.lifeSummary: 當Otton II de Chiny於1065年出生在比利時盧布格尼時，他的父親Arnoul Ier de Chiny30歲，母親Adélaïs de Ramerupt30歲。他於1078年與Adélaïs de Namur結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1125年3月28日在他的家鄉享年60歲，埋葬在比利時盧布格尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Otton II de Chiny于1065年出生在比利时盧布格尼时，他的父亲Arnoul Ier de Chiny30岁，母亲Adélaïs de Ramerupt30岁。他于1078年与Adélaïs de Namur结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1125年3月28日在他的家乡享年60岁，埋葬在比利时盧布格尼。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chiny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Old English cin ‘chin’, as a nickname for someone with a prominent chin or else for a clean-shaven man.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 陳 (see   Chen   1) and 錢 (see   Qian   1).

variants.name: Chin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Wai, Hong, Fook, Chun, Lun, Ying, Young, Yuk, Kin, Leung, Mee.
English (Cornwall): variant of   Chinn  .
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surnames 金, 靳 and 晉, see   Jin   1-3.

variants.name: Ching
meanings: Cornish: dialect form of   Chinn  .
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surnames 荊, 景, 井, 敬, 靖 and 經, see   Jing   1-6.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 程, see   Cheng   1.

variants.name: Chen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Wei, Jian, Ming, Hong, Li, Yan, Wen, Jing, Ping, Ying, Feng, Mei.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 陳, meaning ‘to explain’ and ‘old’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Chen (陳), the name of a state (located mainly in present-day Henan and Anhui provinces) granted to Hu Gong Man (Duke Hu of Chen, reigned 1045–986  BC ). His descendants acquired 陳, the name of the state, as their surname. (ii) from the surname Hou Mo Chen (侯莫陳), borne by people from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. They adopted the surname 陳 after they moved to Luoyang (in present-day Henan province) during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Otton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Otton II de Chiny was born in 1065, in Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgium, his father, Arnoul Ier de Chiny, was 30 and his mother, Adélaïs de Ramerupt, was 30. He married Adélaïs de Namur in 1078. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 28 March 1125, in his hometown, at the age of 60, and was buried in Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Otton II de Chiny est né en 1065, à Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgique, son père, Arnoul Ier de Chiny, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Adélaïs de Ramerupt, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Adélaïs de Namur en 1078. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 28 mars 1125, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Chiny, Luxembourg, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Otton II de Chiny 1065 in Chiny, Luxemburg, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Arnoul Ier de Chiny 30 und seine Mutter Adélaïs de Ramerupt 30. Er hat 1078 Adélaïs de Namur geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 28. März 1125 im Alter von 60 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Chiny, Luxemburg, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Otton II de Chiny è nato il nel 1065 nel Chiny, Lussemburgo, Belgio, il suo padre, Arnoul Ier de Chiny, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Adélaïs de Ramerupt, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Adélaïs de Namur nel 1078. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 28 marzo 1125, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a Chiny, Lussemburgo, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Otton II de Chinyが1065年にベルギールクセンボウルグチニーで生まれた時，父Arnoul Ier de Chinyは30歳，母Adélaïs de Rameruptは30歳であった。1078年にAdélaïs de Namurと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1125年3月28日に故郷で60歳で死去し，ベルギールクセンボウルグチニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Otton II de Chiny(이)가 1065년 벨기에 루세보우르그 친이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Arnoul Ier de Chiny(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Adélaïs de Ramerupt(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1078년 Adélaïs de Namur(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1125년 3월 28일 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 루세보우르그 친이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Otton II de Chiny nasceu em 1065, em Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, seu pai, Arnoul Ier de Chiny, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Adélaïs de Ramerupt, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Adélaïs de Namur em 1078. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 28 de março de 1125, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Otton II de Chiny родился в 1065 г. в местности чини, луксембург, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Arnoul Ier de Chiny было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Adélaïs de Ramerupt, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Adélaïs de Namur в 1078 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 28 марта 1125 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности чини, луксембург, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Otton II de Chiny nació en 1065, en Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica, su papá, Arnoul Ier de Chiny, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Adélaïs de Ramerupt, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Adélaïs de Namur en 1078. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 28 de marzo de 1125, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en Chiny, Luxemburgo, Bélgica.
